summary: PR 2403 changed only src/melee/ft/ftmaterial.c, replacing NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs for ftMaterial_800BF534 and ftMaterial_800BF6BC with C using fighter material/color-overlay TEV setup, and made small local-shape edits in ftMaterial_800BF2B8. The bot report recorded one new match for ftMaterial_800BF534 and score improvements for ftMaterial_800BF6BC and ftMaterial_800BF2B8.

key_files.role: Only changed file; adds ftmaterial implementations and local matching edits.

what_changed.text: Added includes for ft/ftdevice.h, lb/lb_00B0.h, and baselib/debug.h to support fighter device/common data access, color-register helpers, OSReport, and __assert usage.
Adjusted ftMaterial_800BF2B8 local declarations and assignment placement, and changed the PE descriptor branch condition from fp->x2223_b4 to fp->x2223_b3 when fp->x61D == 0xFF.

Implemented ftMaterial_800BF534, which checks ColorOverlay light flags, allocates a TEV color register with lbGetFreeColorRegister, sets HSD_TExp constants from overlay->x50_light_color, configures an HSD_TevDesc, and otherwise calls ftCo_8009F75C(fp, false) when the light-overlay path is not usable.

Implemented most of ftMaterial_800BF6BC, including overlay/player color selection, alpha/color blending calculations, TEV constant setup, two color-register allocations, optional alpha constant setup from fp->x61D, and final HSD_SetupTevStage call.
Automated report recorded ftMaterial_800BF534 as a new 100.00% match (+387 bytes), ftMaterial_800BF6BC improving to 99.47% (+1156 bytes), and ftMaterial_800BF2B8 improving from 99.07% to 99.18%.

smart_moves.text: The PR kept the work focused to one translation unit and one fighter-material area, avoiding broad source or data churn in the loaded diff.

The implementation used existing project rendering helpers and structs such as HSD_TExpSetReg, HSD_StateAssignTev, HSD_SetupTevStage, HSD_TECnst, HSD_TevDesc, lbGetFreeColorRegister, and lb_8000CC8C/lb_8000CCA4 rather than introducing new abstractions.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For fighter material TEV functions, recovering the code may require modeling both the TEV expression chain and the PE/TEV stage setup: color constants, register allocation, and kcsel/kasel selection are all part of the matching surface.

Small declaration-lifetime and local-scope reshaping in a caller can accompany callee implementation work and produce a measurable score improvement, but the loaded evidence only proves a small ftMaterial_800BF2B8 score change, not the author's reason for each local declaration move.

naming_conventions.text: The PR mostly retained address-style function names ftMaterial_800BF534 and ftMaterial_800BF6BC and offset/flag-style fighter fields such as x2223_b2, x2223_b3, x61D, and x610_color_rgba where semantics were not fully named.
Local names are a mix of role names and decompiler-style temporaries: overlay, color_hex, fp_color, reg1/reg2, chk1, var_r0, var_r3, and var_r5.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used a base pointer to ftMObj plus fixed offsets, e.g. base + 0xC4, base + 0x50, base + 0xF4, base + 0x118, and base + 0x128, to copy template HSD_TECnst/HSD_TevDesc data and reference report/assert strings.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is observed PR evidence, not a recommended standard; loaded global standards prefer typed fields and verified data ownership over raw pointer math when recoverable.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Materialized some boolean tests through explicit integer temporaries, such as reg < 4 feeding chk or var_r0 before selecting kcsel/kasel.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The PR evidence shows this shape landed with score improvements, but no review or author comment explains whether it was required for exact codegen.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used raw OSReport and __assert calls with base-derived string pointers for register-allocation failure paths.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Global standards prefer assert/report macros when file, line, message, and side effects can be identified; this PR slice does not include review discussion about macro cleanup.

review_feedback.text: No human review comments or reviews are present in the loaded PR slice.
The only loaded comment is the automated decomp-dev bot report showing one new match and two unmatched-item improvements.

notes: Strong evidence for PR identity, changed file, diff contents, merge timestamp, and bot-reported match/score results.
No PR body text explains intent.
No human review comments are loaded.
The raw PR JSON is truncated, but metadata_json and text_corpus provide the necessary PR identity and comment evidence.
Do not infer exact author rationale for raw pointer math, local declaration movement, raw asserts, or temporary names beyond the code and bot report.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Fighter material TEV matching needs expression-chain and stage-setup fidelity
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2403 shows ftmaterial progress by implementing TEV color-overlay paths with HSD_TECnst/HSD_TevDesc copies, lbGetFreeColorRegister, HSD_TExpSetReg, HSD_StateAssignTev, and HSD_SetupTevStage. The bot reported ftMaterial_800BF534 as a new full match and ftMaterial_800BF6BC as a large improvement.

source_update_candidates.title: Document caution around base-pointer copies of material template data
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2403 used char* base = (char*) &ftMObj and fixed offsets to copy HSD_TECnst/HSD_TevDesc templates and access report/assert strings. Curator may want to note this as an observed matching tactic that should be reviewed against typed-field/data-ownership standards before reuse.
source_update_candidates.reason: The tactic appears prominently in a merged matching PR, but current standards already warn against raw pointer math and fake data ownership; this should be curated as cautionary, not automatically promoted.

source_update_candidates.title: Review raw OSReport/__assert usage in matched code paths
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2403 kept raw OSReport and __assert calls with base-derived string pointers for ftmaterial register-allocation failure paths. Curator may want to compare this with assert/report macro cleanup guidance and decide whether this PR is an exception, bridge code, or a future cleanup target.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded global standard prefers HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTMSG/HSD_ASSERTREPORT forms when identifiable, but the PR evidence lacks review discussion or validation for macro alternatives.

rejection_notes: Do not promote raw ftMObj + offset accesses as accepted style from this PR alone.
Do not infer that the local declaration reshaping or unused/sp100 locals are generally acceptable tactics without explicit objdiff evidence beyond the bot score summary.
Do not claim ftMaterial_800BF6BC fully matched; the bot report says 99.47%, not 100.00%.
Do not infer human reviewer approval details; no human review comments were loaded.
